business.headline: Is the stock market open or closed on Holi, March 4?Wondering if you can trade stocks on Holi this year? While celebrations continue across India on March 4, here's what you need to know about NSE and BSE trading.

India has enough oil stocks, no immediate plan to hike fuel prices: Govt sourcesIndia has 25 days of crude oil and refined oil stocks and is scouting for alternative sources for importing crude oil, LPG and LNG, government sources have confirmed. There is no immediate plan to raise the price of petrol and diesel, according to government sources. However, there is bound to be impact where gas and oil is concerned with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. About 80 per cent of the oil that India depends on comes from Iran and through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz has been shut for the past 48 hours. Energy analyst Chetan Bhutani states that 'strategic reserves can support LPG and LNG demand for about 15 to 20 hot days, if needed, while crude oil reserves are estimated to last about 40 to 45 days in the event of supply disturbances.'

23-year-old turns India's instant house help craze into $100 million startupFrom that single hub in Gurugram to a presence across 10 cities, the growth has been swift. Today, nearly half of Pronto's demand comes from the New Delhi region, with about a fifth from its home base of Bengaluru. The platform now clocks more than 18,000 bookings daily, and over 4,500 professionals worked at least one shift on the app last month.

Iran–US conflict puts India's basmati exports at risk, farmers fear lossesIndia sends nearly 72% of its total basmati exports to Middle Eastern countries. As conflict spreads in the region, uncertainty has started to cloud this important trade. If the war continues for a long time, exports could fall sharply, and farmers may end up paying the price.

India has enough oil stocks for 50 days, looking for more options: Govt sourcesGlobal oil prices rose for the third straight session on Tuesday. Traders are worried that supplies from the Middle East, one of the world's most important oil-producing regions, could be disrupted.

I have Rs 50,000. Should I open a shop or invest in financial assets?Rs 50,000 may not look like a big amount, but it can be the starting point of something meaningful. It could turn into a small business you build with your own hands, or quietly grow through smart investments. The choice you make today could shape your financial future.

Dubai caught in geopolitical storm. Will it hurt its billionaire-friendly image?Dubai has thrived by being the Gulf's safe address and has long promised stability in a turbulent region. Now, with conflict edging closer, that promise is being quietly tested.

India trims gas supply to industries after Qatar halts LNG production: ReportQatar halted its LNG production on Monday as Iran continued strikes in the Gulf in response to Israeli and US attacks. The situation has disrupted energy shipments and pushed up global fuel prices and shipping costs.

Gold jumps nearly Rs 5,000: Will prices continue to rise amid the US-Iran war?The yellow metal is shining once again as geopolitical uncertainty fuels fresh buying interest. As the US-Iran war unsettles global markets, investors are wondering, will gold continue its upward climb from here?

Why is stock market closed today when people are celebrating Holi on March 4?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) have declared March 3 as the official trading holiday for Holi 2026 in their annual calendar.

EPFO interest stays at 8.25%: Why has the rate not increased this year?At a time when households are watching every rupee, even a small rise in EPF returns would have felt welcome. Instead, the rate remains at 8.25%. Here's why the retirement body has chosen stability over a hike.

Oil climbs for third day as US-Israel conflict with Iran threatens supplyThe main concern in the market remains the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil and gas supply passes.

Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

European gas futures jump 42% after Qatar halts LNG amid Middle East conflictEuropean natural gas prices have surged sharply due to the halt in LNG supplies caused by Middle East conflict. This disruption highlights Europe's growing energy vulnerability amid geopolitical tensions.

QatarEnergy to halt LPG production as Middle East tensions deepenThe crisis entered its third day as Iran widened its retaliation following joint US–Israeli strikes that killed its Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with several senior military and political figures.

If 50% of insurers offer mental health cover, why is usage below 20%?While it is encouraging that mental health cover is becoming more common, the low usage rate raises questions. Are employees fully aware of what is available to them? Do they know how to access these services? Or are they still hesitant to seek help?
EPFO's interest stays at 8.25% for FY26The last change in the rate came in 2024, when it was raised from 8.15% to 8.25% for 2023-24. Since then, there has been no further increase or reduction.
Sensex ends 1,048 lower, Nifty below 24,900; RIL down 2%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,048.34 points to close at 80,238.85, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 312.95 points to end at 24,865.70.

From LPG prices to UPI rules: 3 key March 2026 changes that could impact your budgetMarch 2026 has brought a few important changes that could affect your monthly budget. From LPG cylinder prices to new UPI rules and railway updates, here are three key changes you should know about.

Strait of Hormuz is buzzing again and why it matters to the global economyThe Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway between Iran and Oman that connects the Gulf to the Arabian Sea. On a typical day, ships carrying oil equal to about one-fifth of global demand pass through it.

entertainment.headline: Selena Gomez kisses Benny Blanco's dirty feet weeks after his viral podcast. Video Selena Gomez kissed her husband Benny Blanco's foot during a recent podcast appearance. This act was seen as a playful response to online criticism he received over a previous viral video showing his dirty bare feet.

Dry day at box office this Holi: Upcoming Dhurandhar 2 vs Toxic clash to blame? Why are there no big Hindi or South releases this Holi? As India celebrates, the film industry seems to be waiting for March 19, when Dhurandhar: The Revenge takes on Toxic. Is it strategic caution, calendar pressure or a sign that the Holi box office window is losing its sheen?

How did Zendaya's mother react to her wedding news with Tom Holland? Read here Zendaya's mother Claire Stoermer has reacted to reports about her daughter already being married to Tom Holland, after stylist Law Roach claimed that the two actors had secretly tied the knot.

Rashmika-Vijay meet fans in Hyderabad after ancestral village visit. Pics Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda attended a post-wedding fan meet in Hyderabad. The couple greeted fans while returning from a visit to Vijay's ancestral village in Telangana.

Mandana Karimi on moving back to Iran amid conflict: India has betrayed me in a way Mandana Karimi said that she has plans to move back to Iran the moment the current regime is gone. She also opened up on losing friends in Mumbai after she protested against the Iranian government.

Report debunks viral claim of Quentin Tarantino's death in Israel-Iran conflict Widespread online rumours alleging filmmaker Quentin Tarantino died in a missile strike amid Israel-Iran conflict have been debunked by sources close to him, confirming he is unharmed.

Sooraj Barjatya says Hum Aapke Hai Koun isn't possible today. Here's what changed Filmmaker Sooraj Barjatya admits a film like his 1994 blockbuster Hum Aapke Hai Koun isn't possible today. His comment came as he reflected on the changing roles of women on and off the screen.

Singer Bad Bunny breaks Sydney attendance record with Australia debut show Puerto Rican artist Bad Bunny made his live debut in Australia, performing two sold-out shows in Sydney and setting a new attendance record at ENGIE Stadium.

Ayesha Khan on memes over shooting Dhurandhar during periods: Everything is joke Ayesha Khan reacted to the online trolling after she confessed about shooting the Shararat song in Dhurandhar while being on periods. She emphasised the need to educate young boys on such issues.

Dhurandhar's Shararat girl Ayesha Khan says her saying she filmed the song while on periods became a national joke: I wasn't embarrassed

Vicky-Katrina to Varun and Kriti Sanon, Bollywood stars share colourful Holi pics Katrina Kaif, Vicky Kaushal, Varun Dhawan and Kriti Sanon offered fans a look into their Holi festivities by posting vibrant photos and messages online.

No one is giving me any National Awards anymore: Five-time winner Shabana Azmi Five-time National Award winner Shabana Azmi said she no longer expects to win India's top honour for cinematic achievement, ahead of her upcoming film, Lahore 1947. She also revealed what truly drives her as an actor now.

Janhvi Kapoor defends brother Arjun against online bullying: No excuse Janhvi Kapoor discussed the persistent social media harassment targeting her and her half-brother, Arjun Kapoor. In a recent interview, she emphasised that there is no justification for bullying.

Sonu Sood reacts after Rajpal Yadav says he doesn't need work Sonu Sood responded after Rajpal Yadav clarified that he does not need to seek work following his interim bail in a cheque bounce case. Sonu said he never implied Rajpal needed help, but urged producers to sign him and pay advances as a mark of dignity.

Kantara actor Sapthami Gowda slams paps who click invasive pics of female actors Kantara actor Sapthami Gowda publicly spoke out against a section of the paparazzi that clicks and circulates inappropriate videos and photos of female actors at public events. She called for restraint and professionalism.

Priyanka Chopra wishes her father had met husband Nick Jonas and daughter Malti Actor Priyanka Chopra wished to see her late father, Ashok Chopra, meet her husband Nick Jonas and daughter Malti. She said her father would have chosen Nick as her life partner.

The Evil Dead star Bruce Campbell says his cancer is treatable but not curable Actor Bruce Campbell, known for his iconic role in the Evil Dead franchise, has revealed he has been diagnosed with a type of cancer that is non-curable but treatable.

Mammootty calls for peace amid Iran-Israel conflict, highlights hope for humanity Veteran actor Mammootty called for peace amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict, talking about how he wants humanity to prevail as violence intensifies in West Asia.

Anubhav Sinha on Deepika Padukone's 8-hour shift stance: Resolve disputes privately Filmmaker Anubhav Sinha weighed in on fixed working hours in Bollywood, a conversation that was started by actor Deepika Padukone. He advocated for professional agreements between actors and producers.

Saiyami Kher reviews Taapsee Pannu's Assi: One of the most important films Saiyami Kher praised Anubhav Sinha's Assi, saying the Taapsee Pannu-starrer "refuses to leave" her even a week after watching it. She called it one of the most important films made in a long time.

general news.headline: Mojtaba, son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, elected Iran's new Supreme Leader: ReportIran International reports Mojtaba Khamenei elected Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts, allegedly under IRGC pressure, marking the controversial father-to-son succession.

Live: 170 students, teachers killed in strikes on 20 Iran schools, says ministerUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance continues to intensify on Wednesday, the fifth day of direct military confrontation, with no immediate signs of de-escalation. At least 787 people have been killed in Iran, nearly 60 in Lebanon, around a dozen in Israel, and six US soldiers have also died. Israel launched fresh strikes on Iran and renewed attacks on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, including airstrikes in and around Beirut. Iran has retaliated with counterstrikes on Iran and US interests in the Gulf. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the escalating Middle East conflict.

Will UAE strike Iran after wave of attacks on consulate, port and air base?The UAE has dismissed a media report claiming it is weighing participation alongside the US and Israel in military strikes on Iran. For the past five days, Iranian missiles have damaged several key infrastructure, including the US Consulate in Dubai, as part of retaliatory attacks on the US and Israel.

India to buy 5 more Russian S-400s, Op Sindoor hero, amid Middle East conflictAs the Middle East remains on boil following US-Israeli strikes against Iran and subsequent retaliatory attacks by Tehran, India is set to procure five additional squadrons of Russian S-400 air defence missile systems, which played a key role during Operation Sindoor last year.

Israeli plan to make Pak a vassal state, India part of it: Khawaja Asif on Iran warPakistani Defence Minister Khawaja Asif claimed that the conflict between US-Israel and Iran was orchestrated by Zionists to reduce Pakistan into a vasal state. He said Israel's victory would lead to India, Afghanistan and Iran adopting a "joint single point agenda" of enmity towards Pakistan.

How did Iran catch the mightier US and Israel on the back foot?The ongoing conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran has defied early expectations, revealing Iran's unexpected military resilience and strategic depth.

Lakshya Sen starts All England with statement win over defending champion Shi Yu QiLakshya Sen stunned defending champion Shi Yu Qi 23-21, 19-21, 21-17 in a thrilling Round of 32 clash at the All England Championships, avenging his World Championships defeat with a statement win.

Russia offers India a helping hand with energy supplies amid Gulf crisisWith tensions in West Asia threatening global oil supplies, Russia has offered to step in with energy support for India, as per a Russian source quoted by Reuters.

Video: Thick smoke over US Consulate in Dubai after drone hits parking lot amid warA large plume of smoke and fire was seen over the U.S. Consulate in Dubai on Tuesday, as tensions continue to escalate amid the ongoing confrontation involving Iran, the United States and Israel.

Iran prepares for historic funeral of slain Supreme Leader Khamenei in MashhadAyatollah Ali Khamenei was originally from Mashhad, Iran's second-largest city, where his father is buried at the Imam Reza shrine. The senior cleric, who led Iran for 36 years, was killed at the age of 86 during a wave of joint US-Israeli strikes during this past weekend.

I might have forced Israel's hand: Trump denies being pulled into war with IranUS President Donald Trump claims he may have compelled Israel to act against Iran, denying that Israel's planned strike prompted US military action.

Man poses as Isro scientist on matrimony site, dupes Pune techie of Rs 42 lakhA 28-year-old software engineer from Maharashtra was duped of Rs 42.64 lakh by a man posing as an Isro scientist on a matrimonial site, turning her search for a life partner into a nightmare.
Tendulkar family wows in heavy ethnics at Arjun-Saaniya's pre-wedding bashArjun Tendulkar, IPL cricketer and son of Sachin Tendulkar, is all set to marry Saaniya Chandhok on March 5.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

Pahalgam attack probe traces GoPro camera to China, NIA to seek Beijing's assistanceA GoPro camera recovered during the investigation into the April 22, 2025 Pahalgam terror attack – which killed 26 tourists – was activated in China months before the assault.

Touched by God: The many shades of Jasprit BumrahIndia vs England: Jasprit Bumrah has not been used in the T20 World Cup as a traditional spearhead of the bowling attack. India Today delves into how Bumrah has adapted to this new role and where his skills stem from.
The weight of a nation: India's obesity crisis meets public policyA fitter India won't be forged in gyms alone, but in budgets, bylaws and bold political will

37 Indian-flagged ships, 1109 sailors stranded as Strait of Hormuz closesAs fighting intensifies, 37 Indian ships remain stuck near the Strait of Hormuz, with authorities coordinating rescue preparedness and closely tracking maritime security threats.

Indian airlines plan to operate 58 flights to Middle East today: GovtThe ministry is continuously coordinating with airlines, airport operators, regulatory authorities and the external affairs ministry to ensure safe operations, orderly restoration of services and facilitation of affected passengers.

Selena Gomez kisses Benny Blanco's dirty feet weeks after his viral podcast. VideoSelena Gomez kissed her husband Benny Blanco's foot during a recent podcast appearance. This act was seen as a playful response to online criticism he received over a previous viral video showing his dirty bare feet.

Vicky-Katrina to Varun and Kriti Sanon, Bollywood stars share colourful Holi picsKatrina Kaif, Vicky Kaushal, Varun Dhawan and Kriti Sanon offered fans a look into their Holi festivities by posting vibrant photos and messages online.

If weapons unlimited, why rapid build-up order? Trump's contradictory war mathAs Iran continues missile and drone strikes across the region and US interceptors are reportedly under strain, the Trump administration faces mounting scrutiny. If the United States truly has unlimited munitions to fight wars "forever," then why is there a rush to build more?

Iran diplomat accuses US of fuelling wars to block India, China, Russia's riseAbdul Majeed Hakeem Ilahi, Special Representative of the office of Iran's Supreme Leader, asserted that the US wants to maintain global dominance and is uncomfortable with the prospect of a multipolar world order.

Amid Middle East war, X to slap 90-day revenue ban for undisclosed AI videosThe announcement comes against the backdrop of the ongoing crisis in the Middle East after US-Israeli strikes on Iran, which saw social media platforms flooded with fake and misleading AI-generated images and videos.

sports.headline: England's spin edge over India: Vaughn picks favourite for IND vs ENG semi-final T20 World Cup: Michael Vaughan believes England's spin trio could outmatch India in the T20 World Cup semi-final in Mumbai. Despite India entering as favourites, the former captain feels England might spring a surprise.

Touched by God: The many shades of Jasprit Bumrah India vs England: Jasprit Bumrah has not been used in the T20 World Cup as a traditional spearhead of the bowling attack. India Today delves into how Bumrah has adapted to this new role and where his skills stem from.

Abhishek Sharma gets crucial boost from Ponting ahead of England semi final Ricky Ponting has advised Abhishek Sharma to reduce batting practice amid a poor run in the T20 World Cup. Abhishek's form will be key for India ahead of their semi-final clash against England in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Jos Buttler should be left alone: Harry Brook backs England batter before India semis T20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Harry Brook has backed Jos Buttler to bounce back from his lean run of form when England take on India in the semifinal on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium.

Ravi Shastri warns South Africa before semis vs New Zealand: Don't drop your guard T20 World Cup, SA vs NZ: Ravi Shastri said that South Africa need to be on their toes against New Zealand in the all-important semifinal on Wednesday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

In Aiden Markram's clarity, South Africa find balance in T20 World Cup Undefeated South Africa play New Zealand in the first semi-final of the T20 World Cup 2026. South Africa's charge in this tournament has been led by Aiden Markram, who is enjoying the absolute peak of his batting form and captaincy.

Stuart MacGill clashes with podcaster over remarks about David Warner's wife Former Australian spinner Stuart MacGill and host Andrew Menczel had a heated argument on the Cricket Unfiltered podcast. The confrontation arose over abuse faced by Candice Warner during Australia's 2018 South Africa tour and led to personal insults and threats.

Frustrated with Mike Hesson, Aleem Dar resigns from Pakistan selection panel: Report T20 World Cup: Aleem Dar has reportedly resigned from the Pakistan men's selection panel, citing frustration over selection decisions made by head coach Mike Hesson.

New Zealand used to underdogs tag: Mitchell Santner before South Africa semis T20 World Cup, NZ vs SA: Speaking to the reporters, Skipper Mitchell Santner said that New Zealand are happy to go into their semifinal against South Africa on March 4 at the iconic Eden Gardens in Kolkata

Renuka Singh ruled out of pink-ball Perth Test, Kashvee Gautam earns call-up INDW vs AUSW: Renuka Singh has been ruled out of the pink-ball Test against Australia as a part of workload management. All-rounder Kashvee Gautam earned her maiden Test call-up.

Matt Henry doubtful for semi-final vs South Africa, hints Mitchell Santner T20 World Cup: New Zealand take on South Africa in the first semi-final of the T20 World Cup. The team might not have the services of Matt Henry, their best fast bowler in the tournament.

Aiden Markram explains unbeaten South Africa's key to success ahead of NZ semi-finalT20 World Cup, SA vs NZ 1st semi-final: South Africa captain Aiden Markram revealed his team's secret to success in the ongoing tournament. The Proteas are going to semi finals unbeaten and are eyeing their second successive final appearance.

Lunar eclipse forces India to alter training time in Mumbai ahead of semifinalT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Team India shifted their training schedule for the semi-final against England due to a lunar eclipse, during which starting new activities is traditionally considered inauspicious.

Lakshya Sen starts All England with statement win over defending champion Shi Yu QiLakshya Sen stunned defending champion Shi Yu Qi 23-21, 19-21, 21-17 in a thrilling Round of 32 clash at the All England Championships, avenging his World Championships defeat with a statement win.

PV Sindhu recalls Dubai ordeal: Situation was frightening, happy to be back homeIndia badminton star PV Sindhu recalled her ordeal while being stuck at the Dubai Airport due to the US bombing of Iran and subsequent closing of the airspace in the Gulf region. Sindhu, who was on her way to take part in the All England Championships, was left stranded in Dubai since Saturday, February 28.

PV Sindhu on Dubai nightmare: Airports shut, heard interception sounds during crisisPV Sindhu opened up about being stranded at Dubai International Airport during the West Asian military conflict. She recalled being stuck at the airport for over 10 hours.

India men's basketball team stranded in Doha amid airspace restrictionsIndia men's basketball team is stranded in Doha due to ongoing airspace restrictions amid geopolitical tensions. Diplomatic and community efforts are underway to ensure the team's safe return to India.

England wary of Abhishek despite poor form: Hope he doesn't score another 100England all-rounder Sam Curran remains cautious of India opener Abhishek Sharma despite his mixed form. The semi-final promises a high-scoring contest with both teams eager to reach the ICC T20 World Cup final.

India Women's World Cup-winning team nominated for Laureus Awards 2026India Women have been nominated for the Laureus World Team of the Year 2026 at the Laureus Awards following their historic first-ever Women's Cricket World Cup triumph in November 2025.

India women's team slams football body over ill-fitting kits before Asian Cup openerIndia's women's football team exposed AIFF mismanagement after receiving ill-fitting kits a day before their AFC Women's Asian Cup opener against Vietnam in Perth, Australia.

Tennis match in Fujairah halted due to explosions nearby, players forced to leave: VideoThe match between Daniil Ostapenkov and Hayato Matsuoka in the Fujairah Challenger was halted after loud explosions and air-raid sirens were heard near the venue at Fujairah Tennis Country Club.

IPL back at Chinnaswamy Stadium: Bengaluru to host 5 games, playoffs, and finalIPL 2026: The cricketing action is all set to return to the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru as Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) are all set to play their five matches of the season at the venue. The historic stadium will also host two play-off fixtures, including the final of the tournament.

Stick with Abhishek Sharma: Shastri, Ponting defend under-fire India batterT20 World Cup: Ravi Shastri and Ricky Ponting have backed Abhishek Sharma for the semi-final clash against England, urging India not to drop the opener amidst his poor run of form.

Rashid Khan to be sacked? Afghanistan mulls leadership changes after T20 World Cup exitT20 World Cup: Afghanistan are contemplating changes to their leadership structure after a disappointing campaign that saw them exit at the group stage, following defeats to South Africa and New Zealand.

ICC Rankings: Smriti Mandhana climbs to top of Women's ODI batting listIndia opener Smriti Mandhana on Tuesday climbed to the top of the ICC Women's ODI batting rankings following her fine performances in the recently concluded three-match series against Australia.

Gautam Gambhir salutes Rinku Singh's courage for turning up after personal tragedyGautam Gambhir delivered an emotional tribute to Rinku Singh in a team huddle, praising him for rejoining the squad days after his father's death ahead of India's clash against the West Indies.

Abhishek Sharma needs to change mindset: EX-IND batter slams opener after WI flopshowT20 World Cup: Former India batter Manoj Tiwary has asked Abhishek Sharma to tweak his mindset a bit after his slump in form continued in the T20 World Cup 2026. Abhishek was dismissed for 10 off 11 balls in Kolkata.

Father time for Ro-Ko: Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli embrace dad duties, videos go viralRohit Sharma and Virat Kohli are embracing the roles of doting fathers perfectly as the duo were seen spending time with their sons, Ahaan and Akaay, respectively.

Travis Head rejects claims Australia lacked commitment in ICC tournamentICC T20 World Cup: Travis Head has hit back at the public narrative suggesting the Australian team crashed out in the group stage because they do not care as much about the format.

Will India unleash Jasprit Bumrah in powerplay vs ENG? Sunil Gavaskar weighs inIndia should unleash Jasprit Bumrah early in the powerplay against England's top order to break their batting backbone, says Sunil Gavaskar ahead of the T20 World Cup semifinal in Mumbai.

SA vs NZ: Why New Zealand must be wary of in-form Aiden Markram in semi-finalT20 World Cup: Captain Aiden Markram's aggressive starts will be key for South Africa in the first semi-final against New Zealand at Kolkata's Eden Gardens. Markram's sensational run as opener has powered South Africa's unbeaten run.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
